item.title: Version 1.3.0
item.description:
What's new in 1.3.0

  • Open multiple project folders at once — opening a folder in the sidebar no longer closes the others. Each open folder shows its own chats, and closing a folder just tidies the drawer without switching your active project.
  • Tidy chat lists — each folder shows its 5 most recent chats. If there are more, a subtle "See all (N)" row expands the full list in place, with "Show less" to tuck it back away. A chat that's still running always stays visible.
  • The drawer remembers itself — which folders you had open is restored when you relaunch the app.

item.title: Version 1.2.17
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.17

  • Subagents you can actually read — click any agent in the drawer to open a full view with its task, complete live activity, and final result. Text is selectable, and it works side by side with the chat.
  • Subagent results land in the chat — when an agent finishes, its result appears as a clearly marked "Subagent result" card, and the lead agent gets it with your next message.
  • No more lost results — the final answer of every subagent is now captured instead of being thrown away, and activity lines are no longer cut off.

item.title: Version 1.2.16
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.16

  • Each chat remembers its own model — picking a model in one chat no longer changes it in your other chats. Every conversation keeps its own choice (also across app restarts); new chats simply start on the model you used last.

item.title: Version 1.2.15
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.15

  • Fix: the working dot no longer bounces — the little pulsing dot next to "Conjuring…", "Working…" etc. could start bouncing up and down like a ball, especially with two projects working at the same time. Its pulse animation was accidentally grabbing onto layout movement; it's now locked to fading only. Same fix for the yellow dot in the sidebar.

item.title: Version 1.2.14
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.14

  • Fix: no more bouncing chat — the little dot next to the thinking label sat in a chat area that wobbled up and down while working (introduced in 1.2.13 by the new sidebar pulse animation). The chat is steady again; the sidebar dot still pulses.

item.title: Version 1.2.13
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.13

  • Chats keep working when you switch away — a running task no longer looks abandoned when you hop to another chat. Come back and it's still live: streaming text, the working animation, and the red stop button, right where you left them.
  • Pulsing dot in the sidebar — any conversation still working in the background shows a small pulsing yellow dot, so you always know something is cooking.
  • Done notifications always arrive — you now get the finished notification even if you switched to another chat mid-task.
  • Deleting a conversation now also stops any task it was still running.

item.title: Version 1.2.12
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.12

  • Fix: scroll freeze permanently gone — replaced the markdown parser with a custom fast renderer that runs synchronously (no background threads, no re-renders). Switched from LazyVStack to VStack so scroll heights are always exact.
  • Fix: old chats now reliably open at the bottom — waits for layout to settle before scrolling, so you always land on the newest message.

item.title: Version 1.2.11
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.11

  • Fix: scroll freeze actually gone — rows now render at their final height the moment they appear. No more layout jumping, no more main thread stalls during scroll.

item.title: Version 1.2.10
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.10

  • Fix: old chats now open at the bottom — opening a previous chat now correctly lands you at the most recent message.

item.title: Version 1.2.9
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.9

  • Fix: scroll freeze properly solved — all text rendering (markdown parsing, syntax highlighting) now runs on a background thread. The main thread does nothing while rows load during scroll.

item.title: Version 1.2.8
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.8

  • Fix: scroll freeze completely gone — text rendering and syntax highlighting now happen off the main thread as rows load while scrolling. The app stays smooth no matter how long the chat is.

item.title: Version 1.2.7
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.7

  • Fix: long chats no longer freeze — opening an old chat with hundreds of messages is now instant. Only the last 150 messages load on open; a "Show earlier messages" button reveals the rest on demand.
  • Faster scrolling — the chat feed does far less work while you scroll through a long conversation.
  • Typing no longer slows down big chats — keystrokes in the input box no longer re-render the entire message feed.

item.title: Version 1.2.6
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.6

  • Strawberry menu bar icon — the menu bar icon is now the Berries Code strawberry silhouette, and correctly switches between black and white based on your menu bar style.

item.title: Version 1.2.5
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.5

  • Fix: Plugins row alignment — the Plugins sidebar row now lines up exactly with Settings.

item.title: Version 1.2.4
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.4

  • Plugin Store — browse, search, and install Claude Code plugins without touching the terminal. Find it in the sidebar under your projects (puzzle-piece icon). Filter by category, search by name or keyword, and install with one click. Installed plugins show a checkmark and can be uninstalled from the card or the detail view.

item.title: Version 1.2.3
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.3

  • Select text mode — a new "Select text" button at the top-right of the chat turns the conversation into one continuous document, so you can drag-select any part (or all) of a chat and copy it. Copied text comes out labeled You:/Claude: so it stays readable wherever you paste it.
  • Clean chats — internal machinery (skill instructions, plugin/hook notes) no longer shows up inside your conversations.

item.title: Version 1.2.2
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.2

  • Fix: chats disappeared from the sidebar after 1.2.1 — the background-session filter was too broad and also hid chats created by this app. No data was lost; all chats reappear after this update.

item.title: Version 1.2.1
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.1

  • No more phantom chats — background security-review sessions created by Claude Code plugins no longer appear in the sidebar.
  • Freeze fix — the sidebar no longer re-reads entire session files on every refresh; large sessions can't lock up the app anymore.

item.title: Version 1.2.0
item.description:
What's new in 1.2.0

  • Drag & drop images — drop an image anywhere in the app and it attaches as a chip, exactly like the + button. Works with Finder files, screenshot thumbnails, and images dragged from browsers.
  • Dropping an image on the message box no longer pastes the file path as text.
  • A "Drop image to attach" highlight shows while you're dragging.
